title: Bahrain says Iranian drone attack damages water desalination plant
description: Attacks on water systems are uncommon in wartime, but they have appeared in the war in the Middle East with strikes on desalination plants -- critical infrastructure for millions of people in the arid region. Bahrain's interior ministry said Sunday that an Iranian drone attack damaged a water desalination plant, accusing Tehran of "randomly" targeting civilian infrastructure. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis reports.

title: Explosions heard across Doha, sirens sound in Bahrain as Iran strikes Gulf neighbours
description: Several more explosions were heard across Doha and warning sirens sounded in Manama on Monday, as Iran pressed its aerial campaign against Gulf neighbours. The oil-rich Gulf states have been targeted by waves of drones and missiles since Tehran launched its retaliation campaign in the wake of the US and Israeli attacks against Iran. Hoda Abdel-Hamid reports from Doha for FRANCE 24.

title: Israel accused of using white phosphorus in Lebanese residential areas
description: Human Rights Watch has accused Israel of "unlawfully" using white phosphorus over residential parts of southern Lebanon last week. White phosphorus ignites on contact with oxygen, can be used as an incendiary weapon and can cause fires, horrific burns, respiratory damage and death.

title: Macron announces ‘purely defensive’ mission to reopen Strait of Hormuz

title: Tehran shrouded in toxic cloud after US-Israeli strikes on oil deposits

Residents reported the smell of burning lingering in the air, with many saying the sky remained dark even as the sun rose and rain fell over the city. The secretive 56-year-old cleric is only the third supreme leader in the history of the Islamic Republic and has close ties to the paramilitary Revolutionary Guard. description: The UK Government has revealed, supposedly for the first time, that it has established four MRO facilities in Ukraine.

title: Pentagon identifies seventh soldier killed in action during Operation Epic Fury
description: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington succumbed to his injuries on Sunday after a March 1 Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia.

title: Turkey sends six F-16 fighters to Northern Cyprus amid Iran missile threats
description: The deployment adds to the buildup of forces in the region following Iranian drone and missile attacks there.

title: Trial over 2014 Air Algeria plane crash opens in Paris
description: Twelve years ago, 116 people died when an Air Algeria flight from Ouagadougou to Algers crashed. Following years of investigation, an “involuntary manslaughter” trial against the Spanish airline Swiftair, who had leased the aircraft and crew to Air Algeria, opens on Monday before the Paris Criminal Court.

title: G7 leaders to discuss release of strategic oil reserves as crisis looms
description: Finance Ministers from the G7 group of the world's leading economies are meeting on March 9 to discuss a potential release of stocks from Strategic Petroleum Reserves. The talks will be joined by the executive director of the International Energy Agency. The organisation was formed in the wake of the 1973 oil crisis to coordinate to use of member states' reserves, the better to offset supply shocks, like the one currently slamming commodities mar

title: 'This is all about defence': Macron addresses European security in Cyprus
description: "When Cyprus is attacked it is Europe that is attacked" said French President Emmanuel Macron as he spoke during a visit to Cyprus after it was targeted by Iranian-made drones. He also mentioned that France and its partners are preparing a 'purely defensive' mission to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more about Europe's reaction to the conflict in the Middle East.

title: Replay: Emmanuel Macron addresses Iran threat to EU member Cyprus
description: Rewatch as French President Emmanuel Macron addresses the threat posed by Iran to Cyprus, an EU member.

title: Europe rallies around Cyprus after Iranian strikes
description: French President Emmanuel Macron travelled to Cyprus on March 9, days after dispatching a warship to the east Mediterranean island nation, where a Shahed drone struck a British air base on its southern coast last week during the Iran war. The goal is to show the 'French military might' to defend partners in the region, among other messages, says FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani.

title: Concerns grow over humanitarian situation in Lebanon
description: Lebanese officials say nearly 400 people have been killed and more than half a million displaced amid fresh Israeli strikes on Beirut and in the south of the country. In the meantime, Hezbollah says it is fighting Israeli forces that have landed in eastern Lebanon by helicopter, while the Israeli army says two of its soldiers have been killed in southern Lebanon.

title: Trump trying to gauge whether new ayatollah is 'a leader he can work with'
description: Iran's council of 88 clerics appointed Mojtaba Khamenei, son of late Ayatallah Ali Khamenei, as new supreme leader of the country. This as US-Israeli strikes keep pounding the country, targeting oil, civilian and military infrastructure. Now, US President Donald Trump is 'gauging' whether the new ayatollah is 'a leader he can work with', Nega Angha, Visiting Fellow at King's College London, tells FRANCE 24.

title: Khamenei replaces Khamenei: Iran defies Trump, signals continuity
description: Iran’s choice of Mojtaba Khamenei to replace his slain father, Ali Khamenei, as supreme leader signals the entrenchment of hardline cleric power and a continued resistance amid the US-Israeli military onslaught. It also underscores US President Donald Trump’s policy failure to detail the goals of the war.

title: US and Gulf states turn to Ukraine to counter Iranian drone attacks
description: The supply of weaponry and drone interceptors could shape the future of the war with Iran, as the US and Gulf nations are trying to find cheaper ways to intercept Iranian drones, before they hit their targets. And it's actually Ukraine actually may have role to play here.

title: Children in Lebanon 'paying the highest price' amid conflict in Middle East: UNICEF
description: One week after hostilities resumed between Israel and Hezbollah, UNICEF has sounded the alarm over the situation for children in Lebanon. According to the health ministry, more than 80 children have been killed in the fighting. Christophe Boulierac, UNICEF Lebanon’s chief of advocacy and communication, told FRANCE 24 that Lebanese children have suffered “deep psychological and emotional scars” that have accumulated since clashes between Israel an

title: War and creativity: Lebanese artists reflect on crisis
description: As the conflict in the Middle East engulfs Lebanon, with air strikes and evacuation orders once again shaking the foundations of society, we hear from Wissam Charaf and Zeid Hamdan, two Lebanese artists grappling with this moment of crisis. Filmmaker Wissam Charaf joins us by video link from Beirut to reflect on the past seven years and how they have driven many artists to flee the country.

title: As Khamenei son takes over, Nigerian Shias mourn Iran’s old supreme leader
description: In northern Nigeria, Shias see new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei as a continuation of his father’s resistance.

description: Iran’s deputy health minister, Dr Ali Jafarian, says more than 1,255 people have been killed and that most are civilians

description: As toxic clouds and rain smother Tehran, analysts say Israel’s aims is to break the resilience of the Iranian people.

description: Expanding regional conflict shifts global attention as Gaza faces border closures, inflation and ceasefire violations.

description: Across Asia, where countries are highly exposed to rising oil and gas costs and tightening supply, governments are acting to mitigate economic harm.

description: Video shows the Precision Strike Missile being launched in open desert terrain from M142 HIMARS as part of Operation Epic Fury.

title: Iran goes ‘full dynasty’ with choice of new supreme leader
description: Iran named Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, a son of the country's late supreme leader, the Islamic Republic’s new ruler on Monday. Rouzbeh Parsi from Sweden’s Lund University said the choice of "going full dynasty" was not surprising, even for a regime that overthrew the Pahlavi monarchy in 1979 since "revolutions tend to replace what they destroyed with something very similar".

title: Many in Lebanon can't afford moving to safer areas as Israeli strikes continue
description: As Israeli strikes intensified in Lebanon on the second week of the conflict, hundreds of thousands of people have been displaced in areas targeted by the attacks. Rents have exploded, with many not being able to afford going to safer areas. FRANCE 24's Renée Davis spoke to residents on the ground.

title: Concern 'inherited power exchange may not go down well' in Iran

title: China calls for end of conflict in Middle East amid global instability
description: China said that Iran's decision to name Mojtaba Khamenei as its new supreme leader following the killing of his father was a domestic matter, and it opposed any attempt to target him. The conflict has threatened global energy security and trade, as well as China's oil supplies. An instability that the economic giant does not see fit to its interests, as FRANCE 24's Yena Lee reports from Beijing.

title: Hezbollah reports clash with overnight Israeli raid in east Lebanon
description: Militant group Hezbollah said on March 9 its fighters clashed with Israeli troops in eastern Lebanon ​during an Israeli airborne raid overnight, in what would be the second such operation in the area in recent days. Meanwhile half a million Lebanese have been displaced and 400 were killed as a result of Israeli strikes. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris Trent has more from Beirut.

title: Israeli Air Force Strikes Iranian F-14 Tomcat Fleet at Isfahan Air Base
description: Iranian F-14 fleet attacked at a base in central Iran believed to host all the remaining F-14s in Iranian service. title: Finland readies itself to be able to host nukes in longtime policy departure
description: The proposed law change would bring the country in line with NATO's nuclear-deterrence architecture, the government argues.

title: European NATO nations reduce reliance on US arms imports: SIPRI data
description: European governments are spending more defense money in South Korea, France and Israel, according to a new analysis.

title: War in the Middle East live: Explosions heard over Tel Aviv as Israel strikes Beirut suburbs, Tehran
description: Israeli air defence systems were working to intercept strikes over Tel Aviv on Monday, the military said, as Israel launched its own strikes on both the southern suburbs of Beirut and the Iranian capital. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

title: Israelis divided over war with Iran as conflict expands
description: Air raid sirens ring out across Israel, alerting residents of Iranian missile launches, a sound that now dictates the daily lives of Israelis. The population has grown accustomed to taking shelter as soon as they hear it. But it doesn't stop some in Jerusalem from enjoying outdoor activities and when it comes to the war, opinions are divided.

title: Iran-related oil price surge impacts United States
description: Crude oil prices have hit their highest levels since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022. International benchmark brent crude jumped some 20% to more than $120 per barrel on March 9. The spike in energy prices will be felt across the global economy - notably in Asia, which imports most of its oil from the Middle East.  But - despite being the world's top oil producer and a net exporter, the US is not immune, as American drivers are no

title: Half a million displaced in Lebanon amid intensifying Israeli strikes
description: An air strike hit Beirut's southern suburbs on March 9 after Israel warned it would target branches of a firm linked to Hezbollah. In the past week, nearly 400 people have been killed in attacks, according to Lebanese authorities. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris Trent breaks it down for us from Beirut.

title: With new supreme leader, Iran says 'business back as usual'
description: Iran has named its new supreme leader, late Ali Khamenei's son Mojtaba Khamenei. The new Ayatollah is considered a fellow hardliner who will pursue his father's rejection of dissent. 'By naming him, Iran shows business is back as usual' says FRANCE 24's Philip Turle.

title: Who is Khamenei's son, the new Iranian supreme leader?
description: After over a week of deliberation Iran's Assembly of experts has chosen Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the late Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as the new supreme leader of the Islamic Republic. The 88-member panel of clerics chose Khamenei with the support of the Revolutionary Guard. As supreme leader, he is expected to echo his father's hardline stances against the West during this time of war for the country.

title: Oil prices hit historic high after Iran names new supreme leader
description: Oil prices soared to nearly $120 per barrel on Monday after Iran announced the nomination of a new supreme leader, before falling to $106.23 per barrel. The choice of Mojtaba Khamenei ‌as the new leader – signalling a continuation of hardliners in power – and fears of prolonged Strait of Hormuz shipping disruptions have pummeled financial markets.

title: Gulf countries targeted by fresh Iranian attacks as repatriation still underway
description: Iran has launched a new wave of missile and drone attacks on Gulf neighbours hosting US forces after Iranian president Masoud Pezeshkian's initial apology to neighbouring countries on March 7 was pushed back by Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps. The UAE, Kuwait, Qatar, Bahrain, Oman, Saudi Arabia and Iraq have all reported drone or missile attacks over the past week.

title: Khamenei son nomination met with mixed reactions
description: Iranian state TV announced that Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the country’s late supreme leader, has been named his successor. He had long been considered a contender, even before an Israeli strike killed his father at the start of the war. U.S. President Donald Trump told ABC News he wants a say in who comes to power once the war is over and he said a new leader “is not going to last long” without his approval.

title: The 'shadow prince': Mojtaba Khamenei succeeds father as Iran's new Supreme Leader
description: PRESS REVIEW – Monday, March 9: The new Supreme Leader embodies both continuity with his father’s hard-line rule and a departure from what was intended to be a non-hereditary system of governance. In other news, Greenpeace has warned of “rogue companies trying to sidestep international law” in the deep seas, while the BBC has published photos from a recent expedition to the Caribbean’s deep waters. description: Разведка США полагает, что у Ирана мог остаться доступ к запасам обогащенного урана на объекте в Исфахане, который, как считалось, был уничтожен во время американских ударов в июне 2025 года. Об этом сообщает The New York Times со ссылкой на источники.

title: США эвакуируют дипломатический корпус из Саудовской Аравии
description: Госдепартамент распорядился начать эвакуацию дипломатической миссии США в Саудовской Аравии, сообщает The New York Times со ссылкой на источники. Решение последовало после ударов Ирана по территории королевства.

title: ‘Country’ Joe McDonald, ’60s US rock star, proud protest counterculture icon, dies at 84
description:
Named after Stalin by his once-Communist Jewish mother and Presbytarian father, anti-war activist starred at Woodstock after rising to fame with 'I-Feel-Like-I’m-Fixin’-To-Die Rag'
The post Country Joe McDonald, 60s US rock star, proud protest counterculture icon, dies at 84 appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: More hardline than his father, Mojtaba Khamenei’s appointment signals defiance and revenge
description:
Successor and son of Iran's slain supreme leader will 'have no choice' but to expand repression, even if war ends, official says, warning world 'will miss the era of his father'
The post More hardline than his father, Mojtaba Khameneis appointment signals defiance and revenge appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Iran names Khamenei’s shadowy, hardline son Mojtaba as new supreme leader
description:

title: Trump to Times of Israel: It’ll be a ‘mutual’ decision with Netanyahu regarding when Iran war ends
description:
US president, in phone interview, clarifies that he'll make final call to end operation 'at right time'; says he and PM ‘worked together' against Islamic Republic: 'We’ve destroyed a country that would have destroyed Israel'

title: Woman injured from flying debris in central Israel as Iran keeps up missile fire
description:

title: NORAD Intercepts Two Russian Tu-142s in Alaskan and Canadian ADIZs
description: F-35s, F-22s and CF-18, supported by tankers and AWACS, were scrambled by NORAD to intercept two Russian Tu-142 operating in the Alaskan and Canadian Air Defense Identification Zones. For the second time this year, the North American Aerospace Defense Command (NORAD) reported an intercept of Russian military aircraft, specifically two Russian Tupolev Tu-142 maritime reconnaissance []

title: Largest US military hospital abroad halts labor, delivery services amid Iran war
description: The Landstuhl Regional Medical Center in Germany is pausing its labor and delivery services to focus on its “primary objective,” a memo states.

title: War in Middle East live: Asian stocks tumble, oil prices soar as war threatens shipping
description: Oil prices eclipsed $115 per barrel on Monday as the Iran war intensified, threatening production and shipping in the Middle East. The price for a barrel of Brent crude, the international standard, surged to $115.31, up 24% from its Friday closing price of $92.69. On Sunday, Iran's Assembly of Experts on Sunday named Mojtaba Khamenei to replace his late father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, as the country's new supreme leader. Follow our liveblog for t

title: Iran war live: Mojtaba Khamenei named supreme leader; Israel bombs Tehran
description: Irans military and political leaders pledge allegiance to Mojtaba Khamenei as US-Israeli attacks continue.

title: Oil prices surge above $110 and shares slide over Iran war
description: Major disruption to energy supplies threatens to push up prices for consumers and businesses around the world.

title: Oil soars past $100 a barrel as US-Israel war on Iran rages
description: Crude oil prices surge more than 20 percent as sprawling regional conflict threatens global energy supplies.

title: In Russia’s Former Capital, Restoring the Past to Survive the Present
description: With political activism off-limits, residents of St. Petersburg are finding purpose and community in the “politics of small deeds,” repairing and cleaning architectural treasures.

title: Госдепартамент США приказал дипломатам срочно покинуть Саудовскую Аравию
description: Персоналу дипломатической миссии США в Саудовской Аравии было приказано покинуть страну, сообщает New York Times со ссылкой на осведомленные источники.

title: Citing conflict of interest concerns, Justice Ministry won’t hand Sde Teiman leak probe to AG
description:

title: Arraba mayor and his deputy shot and injured amid unrelenting crime wave
description:

title: India dismisses reports of US naval attacks on Iran from Indian ports
description:

title: Iran can still fire drones and missiles — experts weigh the implications on the war

title: Trump vows control over Iran leader as US soldiers’ deaths rise to seven
description: Trump says new supreme leader will not last long without US approval, hours before Mojtaba Khamenei is selected.

title: For Xi, Trump’s Embrace of War Proves China Needs More Power
description: China is learning lessons from the war in Iran. Chief among them, the United States may pose an even greater threat than Beijing thought.
